summary: The articles from the specified RSS source cover a range of topics related to technology and artificial intelligence. The first article discusses Mark Zuckerberg's decision to create a new applied AI engineering company and reorganize key teams. This move is part of Facebook's broader strategy to enhance its AI capabilities and stay competitive in the rapidly evolving tech landscape. The second article, 'Open Weights Training is Not Open Training,' delves into the nuances of open-source machine learning models, specifically addressing the misconception that sharing model weights equates to sharing the entire training process. The author emphasizes the importance of transparency in the training methodology and data to ensure reproducibility and ethical use of AI models. The third article is a tweet about an SSH hidden menu, which provides a brief insight into a less-known feature of the Secure Shell (SSH) protocol, suggesting that it includes hidden commands or options that users can explore for additional functionality.
highlights: Mark Zuckerberg is creating a new applied AI engineering company and reorganizing teams to enhance Facebook's AI capabilities
Sharing model weights does not equate to open training; transparency in training methodology and data is crucial
SSH hidden menu offers additional commands or options for users
